zh: 你说是 AI 正在如何改变产品工作的形态。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're working at maybe the most bleeding edge, AI-pilled software team there is.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 所以你有一个非常有趣的视角来看事情的发展方向，以及其他团队在一两年或更久之后会处于什么位置。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does the shape of product team look like now versus a couple years ago?
zh: 产品团队的形态现在与几年前相比是什么样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the hardest things to do right now as a leader building these products is just sort of the inversion of the process in my mind, which I think a lot of people have talked about, which is that anybody can build anything.
zh: 现在作为构建这些产品的领导者，最难做的事情之一就是我脑海中对流程的倒置，我想很多人都谈到过这一点，就是任何人都可以构建任何东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I generally believe now that starting from scratch, if you talk to these models, ours, anybody else's really, you can stand up whatever feature you want.
zh: 我现在普遍相信，从零开始，如果你与这些模型交谈，我们的，或其他人的，你都可以建立起你想要的任何功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's not necessarily a hard part of software, but that's really cool.
zh: 这不一定是软件的难点，但这真的很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that has created an environment where people are making all of this.
zh: 而且我认为这创造了一个人们正在制造所有这一切的环境。

speaker: Unknown
en: You give people unlimited tokens.
zh: 你给人们无限的 tokens。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everybody at OpenAI is very agentic, has great ideas.
zh: OpenAI 的每个人都非常有 agent 性，都有很棒的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so everybody's building everything.
zh: 所以每个人都在构建一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas I think you look back at product process that we've all run for a long time and it's been a little bit opposite.
zh: 而我认为你回顾一下我们要么长期运行的产品流程，它一直有点相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's been kind of research, ideation, maybe there was some prototyping.
zh: 它一直是某种研究，构思，也许有一些原型制作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it was even when we got past waterfall, it was still kind of flavored of the implementation is expensive.
zh: 但即使在我们度过了瀑布式开发之后，它仍然带有实现是很昂贵的这种色彩。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so what you want to do is you want to de-risk all implementation upfront through documents, through research, through prototypes.
zh: 所以你想要做的是，你想通过文档、通过研究、通过原型预先降低所有实现的风险。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because prototypes and designs are cheaper was kind of the assumption there.
zh: 因为原型和设计更便宜，这基本上是那里的假设。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's changed.
zh: 而这已经改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's totally changed.
zh: 这完全改变了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And right now I'm sure there are 90 different explorations for...
zh: 而现在我确信有 90 种不同的探索用于……

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this feature that we desperately need to do that I'm sure there are 90 different uncoordinated teams implementing and trying, right?
zh: 有我们迫切需要做的这个功能，我确信有 90 个不同的不协调的团队正在实施和尝试，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I guess the short answer is it's backwards.
zh: 所以我想简短的回答是它是反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not that people are doing fundamentally different roles or focusing on different things or that even skillsets have vanished or that roles have just disappeared.
zh: 这并不是人们在做根本不同的角色或关注不同的事情，甚至技能集已经消失，或者角色已经消失了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that it's backwards.
zh: 而是它是反的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The implementation is actually not the expensive part anymore.
zh: 实现实际上不再是昂贵的部分了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's, dare I say, taste.
zh: 是，我敢说，品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's the curation process.
zh: 但它是策展过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like of those 90 attempts, what's good about these?
zh: 就像在那 90 次尝试中，这些有哪些好的地方？

speaker: Unknown
en: What should we fold into other aspects of this?
zh: 我们应该把这些折叠进这个的其他方面吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: How should we frame this?
zh: 我们应该如何构建这个？

speaker: Unknown
en: Should it be part of this other feature?
zh: 它应该是这个其他功能的一部分吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many segments should be in the toggle?
zh: 切换里应该有多少段？

speaker: Unknown
en: All of those things.
zh: 所有这些事情。

en: It's essentially Stripe for enterprise features.

en: Visit workos.com to get started.

en: Or just hit up their Slack where they have actual engineers waiting to answer your questions.

speaker: Unknown
en: WorkOS allows you to build faster with delightful APIs, comprehensive docs, and a smooth developer experience.
zh: WorkOS 允许你通过令人愉悦的 API、全面的文档和流畅的开发者体验更快地构建。

speaker: Unknown
en: Go to workos.com to make your app enterprise ready today.
zh: 访问 workos.com 今天让你的应用为企业做好准备。

speaker: Unknown
en: Taste, such a buzzword.
zh: 品味，真是一个流行词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to come back to that.
zh: 我想回到那一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: This idea of 90 prototypes, so interesting.
zh: 90 个原型的想法，太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So just to make sure I understand that.
zh: 所以只是为了确保我理解了这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's an idea out there floating around OpenAI.
zh: 所以有一个想法在 OpenAI 周围流传。

speaker: Unknown
en: What people used to do is write docs.
zh: 人们过去常做的是写文档。

speaker: Andrew Ambrosino
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's what we're going to build, here's the feature, here's the strategies, PRD.
zh: 这就是我们要构建的，这是功能，这是策略，PRD。

speaker: Unknown
en: Today, what you're describing, which makes all the sense, is people just create a prototype.
zh: 今天，你所描述的，这很有道理，是人们只是创建一个原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what you're saying is people across the company have kind of similar ideas.
zh: 而你所说的是，全公司的人都有类似的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now instead of a doc, they create their little prototype.
zh: 现在不是写文档，他们创建了他们的小原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that leads to kind of 90 different things people can look at and maybe pick here's a direction we want to go down.
zh: 这导致了人们可以看看的 90 种不同的东西，也许可以选择这是我们要下去的一个方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the idea?
zh: 是这个主意吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of this.
zh: 有很多这样的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's not just happening here.
zh: 而且这不仅仅发生在这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've seen many product leaders say PRDs are dead and prototypes are in.
zh: 你看到许多产品负责人说 PRD 已死，原型当道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I actually don't believe this at all.
zh: 而我实际上根本不相信这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that one of the interesting things that is happening right now is that because implementation has gotten so cheap across every medium, it's very tempting to jump straight to a prototype.
zh: 我认为现在发生的有趣事情之一是，因为实现在各种媒介上都变得如此廉价，直接跳到原型是非常诱人的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially if you're not an engineer.
zh: 如果你不是工程师的话尤其如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially if you've never been able to write code or never been interested or never had the time, it's really tempting to say like, "PRDs are dead.
zh: 如果你从未能够写代码，或者从未感兴趣，或者从未有时间，说这样的话真的很诱人：“PRD 已死。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me just show you what I mean."
zh: 让我直接向你展示我的意思。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What I've also noticed though is that for engineers, it's really tempting to write a lot of documents, a lot of documents that are not worth reading.
zh: 但我也注意到，对于工程师来说，写很多文档是非常诱人的，写很多不值得读的文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is no shade on people writing documents.
zh: 这不是在影射写文档的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that if implementation is abundant, then it's really important to pick the right format for the point you're trying to make.
zh: 而是说，如果实现是很充裕的，那么为你试图表达的要点选择正确的格式就非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: If that point is product clarity around a vague area, then it might actually be a document.
zh: 如果那个要点是围绕一个模糊领域的产品清晰度，那么它实际上可能是一个文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: If what you're trying to do is get something in people's hands to try out and to stress test an interaction pattern, it's a prototype.
zh: 如果你试图做的是把某样东西放到人们手中去尝试，并对一种交互模式进行压力测试，那就是原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think this is kind of the funny thing now, which is that it's really important to pick the medium.
zh: 但我认为这现在有点像个有趣的事情，也就是选择媒介非常重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this term that a podcast guest shared that I think about when you say this, which is it's called the primal mark.
zh: 有一个播客嘉宾分享的术语，当你这样说时我会想到，它叫做原始印记。

speaker: Unknown
en: When a designer or a painter or an artist just creates the first mark on a painting or a piece of art, that mark is what you start to respond to.
zh: 当设计师或画家或艺术家只是在绘画或艺术品上做出第一个标记时，那个标记就是你开始回应的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so everything kind of trickles down from that first mark you make.
zh: 所以一切都顺着你做出的第一个标记涓滴而下。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I'm hearing you saying is sometimes the prototype is the wrong first thing to do because then you're just responding to this prototype versus a different idea versus a bigger idea.
zh: 而我从你的话中听到的是，有时原型是错误的第一步要做的事情，因为那样你只是在回应这个原型，而不是一个不同的想法，而不是一个更大的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I love hearing this.
zh: 所以我很喜欢听到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So everyone's just like, "Okay, forget it.
zh: 所以大家都像是，“好吧，算了吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: No more writing, no more docs, no more PRDs."
zh: 不再写东西，不再写文档，不再写 PRD。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You're saying, "They're actually still useful for specific use cases."
zh: 你说的是，“它们实际上对于特定用例仍然有用。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think too, there's this part of the previous world was that the medium implied it had baked in a lot of signal around where in the process something was.
zh: 我也认为，以前世界有一部分是，媒介暗示它在流程中某物处于什么位置上 baked in 了很多信号。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you're seeing something that feels like the app in production, that means that it's late in the process.
zh: 所以如果你看到某种感觉像是生产环境中的应用程序的东西，那意味着它在流程中处于后期。

speaker: Unknown
en: That assumptions have been de-risked, that design has looked at this, that this is a good business goal.
zh: 假设已经去风险化了，设计已经看过了，这是一个好的商业目标。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now those things are sort of divorced.
zh: 现在这些事情有点分家了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason it was that way is because it was hard to get resources to build the thing until it was properly de-risked.
zh: 之所以那样是因为，在恰当地去风险化之前，很难获得资源来构建这个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now that's just out the window.
zh: 现在那都抛到九霄云外了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think it's really important to start saying, "Look, we can have prototypes.
zh: 所以我认为开始说非常重要，“看，我们可以有原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: We can have documents.
zh: 我们可以有文档。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are we clear around what this is doing?"
zh: 我们对这是在做什么清楚了吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because to your point, you do not want to over anchor on this thing that was meant to be an exploration, but now it looks so production ready that, oh, visually it's ready for prod.
zh: 因为正如你的观点，你不想过度锚定在这个本意是探索的东西上，但现在它看起来太像生产就绪了，以至于哦，视觉上它准备好上线了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's not actually the right model of where the research is going or what users are asking for or what's right for the business.
zh: 但它实际上不是研究走向或用户要求或业务适合的正确模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not to overdo the taste thing, but it's once again, it's like the taste to know what to work on, how to present that information, how to achieve the goals, what medium to use is emerging as the most important thing to do.

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

zh: 感觉品味是其中的一部分。

speaker: Andrew Ambrosino
en: Yeah.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 很少有像，“就是这个，他们搞定了。”

speaker: Unknown

zh: 而且总是像，“哦，这是 Claude Design，这是 Codex Design。”

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown

zh: 我认为设计比软件更难评分。

speaker: Unknown

zh: 并且创建一个你可以训练模型的循环，什么是好的设计，什么是坏的设计，比代码是否编译、是否做它该做的更乏味和繁重。

speaker: Unknown

zh: 因为品味的人性化部分是你需要的反馈机制的一部分。

speaker: Unknown

zh: 我也认为实验室历史上投资于让他们的模型擅长加速 AI 研究的事情。

speaker: Unknown

zh: 而且在早期编码模型时代，很清楚模型能够编写正确的代码会加速研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: In a way that you can't really make the same case for design.
zh: 而对于设计，你无法真正提出同样的论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not that getting good at design isn't important, it's that it's not directly in that flywheel.
zh: 不是说擅长设计不重要，而是它不直接在那个飞轮中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those are practical reasons and those will go away.
zh: 这些是实际原因，它们会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: These models will get pretty good at design.
zh: 这些模型会变得相当擅长设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are some kind of murkier things that is going to be really tough.
zh: 有一些更模糊的事情将真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have kind of a short list of them.
zh: 我有一个简短的清单。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is there is an aspect of culture to what is considered good design.
zh: 其中之一是，被认为好的设计有一个文化层面。

speaker: Unknown
en: In that you remember it was probably what last year where every new website that came out was just a copy of Linear's website.
zh: 也就是说你记得可能是去年，每一个新推出的网站都只是 Linear 网站的复制品。

speaker: Unknown
en: Linear's website, great design, great taste.
zh: Linear 的网站，很棒的设计，很好的品味。

speaker: Unknown
en: If a model did that, I'd be like, "Wow, this is incredible leaps here."
zh: 如果模型那样做，我会说，“哇，这里是巨大的飞跃。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If I have a model that outputs Linear's website every time, that's not the challenge here.
zh: 如果我有一个每次都输出 Linear 网站的模型，那不是这里的挑战。

zh: 设计中有一种比软件工程实际上更重要的新颖性数量。

speaker: Unknown
en: Software engineering, you must want it to over-index unknown patterns.
zh: 软件工程，你必须希望它过度索引未知模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whereas design, it's like, "No, there's an element of randomness here and novelty."
zh: 而设计，就像，“不，这里有一个随机性和新颖性的元素。”

speaker: Unknown
en: To me, I spent a lot of time writing code or supervising code on the early Codex app.
zh: 对我来说，我花了很多时间编写代码或在早期 Codex 应用上监督代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: And even as the models get good at design, there's sort of an abstraction layer that is an interplay between the software design and the code that's being written.
zh: 即使模型变得擅长设计，也有一种抽象层是软件设计和正在编写的代码之间的相互作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: This thing over here in this corner should share X, Y, and Z in the code base with this thing down here.
zh: 这里角落里的这个东西应该在代码库中与下面这个东西共享 X、Y 和 Z。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a little bit different than saying the model needs to be a better designer.
zh: 这与说模型需要成为一个更好的设计师有点不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially that is visual design, but it is significantly deeper.
zh: 特别是视觉设计，但它要深得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about the abstractions in that like, "Oh, if tomorrow our company did a rebrand, the shallow version of this is that we have to update 263 components one by one."
zh: 这关乎其中的抽象，比如，“噢，如果我们明天公司要重塑品牌，浅层的版本是我们必须逐一更新 263 个组件。”

speaker: Unknown

speaker: Unknown
en: They're both in list that have this style, that convey this interaction pattern to the user.

speaker: Unknown

zh: 我认为有了当前的技术，那个抽象层仍然有点遥不可及。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think as we've gone through this process of, we started the Codex app in November.

speaker: Unknown
en: And we weren't using it full-time, now we use it for everything.
zh: 我们并不是全天候使用它，现在我们用它来做一切事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's been a journey.
zh: 这是一段旅程。

speaker: Unknown
en: But now it's like the things that we actually do while using it are different things.
zh: 但现在就像是，我们在使用它时实际做的事情是不同的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what was the question?

speaker: Unknown
en: I know, that was an amazing answer.

speaker: Unknown
en: Speaking of design and being creative, the Codex app when it came out, it's like such a new thing that nobody had seen before.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not a terminal thing, it's not an ID thing.

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like this chat thing that codes and you could see code.
zh: 它就像这个聊天的东西，它能写代码，你可以看到代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: To your point, it feels like it'd be hard for AI to be like, "Here's a whole new paradigm for how to code."
zh: 照你的观点，AI 感觉很难做到像是，“这里有一个全新的编码范式。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that feels like where human brains continue to be valuable for now is creativity almost in coming up with something new versus patterns of things that have been done before.
zh: 感觉就像人脑目前仍然有价值的地方在于创造力，几乎是提出新东西，而不是以前做过的东西的模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I mean, I totally hear you.
zh: 是的，我的意思是，我完全听到你说的了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's give it up for the human brain.
zh: 让我们为人脑鼓掌。

speaker: Unknown
en: For now.
zh: 目前为止。

speaker: Unknown
en: As we were getting ready for this, you said that you were listening to the episode with Jenny who does the head of design for Claude Code and Cowork and such.
zh: 当我们要为此做准备时，你说你在听那一集，Jenny 她是 Claude Code 和 Cowork 等的设计主管。

speaker: Unknown
en: And she had this whole kind of thesis that the design process is dead.
zh: 她有这整套关于设计过程已死的论点。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no time for design.
zh: 没有时间进行设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: Things are moving too fast.
zh: 事情发展太快了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just build now and design is kind of steering things as things move along.
zh: 现在直接构建，设计是在事情发展过程中进行引导。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're implying you have kind of a different perspective on the design process.
zh: 你暗示你对设计过程有不同的看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: We probably agree on a lot of this, Jenny and I. I wasn't a fan of the design.
zh: 我们可能在很多方面都达成一致，Jenny 和我。我不是设计的粉丝。

speaker: Unknown
en: The design process proper, I agree with her take that it is dead.
zh: 正统的设计过程，我同意她的看法，它已经死了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I genuinely was not a fan of this process before AI.
zh: 在 AI 之前，我真的不是这个过程粉丝。

speaker: Unknown
en: Can you describe the process real quick just from people think about the design?
zh: 你能快速描述一下这个过程吗，就从人们思考设计的角度？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that mean?
zh: 那是什么意思？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I mean, when I ran a startup a number of years ago, we would do design hiring.
zh: 我的意思是，几年前我经营一家初创公司时，我们会做设计招聘。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there was this sort of snarky article that came out about the case study factory.
zh: 有一刻薄的文章是关于案例研究工厂的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was like mid-SERP era stuff and it was that designers were being taught about this process and valuing that above all else, above all outcomes even.
zh: 那就像是 mid-SERP 时代的东西，设计师们被教导这个过程，并将其看得高于一切，甚至高于所有结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if something went through this process that two things were true.
zh: 如果某样东西经历了这个过程，两件事是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: One, it would be good and the process would guarantee quality and guarantee impact.
zh: 第一，它会很好，这个过程会保证质量并保证影响力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also that if something, the thing was good if it went through that process, even if you don't like it and nobody uses it.
zh: 还有，如果某样东西，那个东西是好的，如果它经历了那个过程，即使你不喜欢它，也没人使用它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like the process of user research and the divergence and the convergence, it's the right framework.
zh: 就像是用户研究和发散和收敛的过程，它是正确的框架。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was always a little academic.
zh: 它总是有点学术化。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think this is really exposing some areas where it falls down, especially because of the speed of implementation.
zh: 但我认为这确实暴露了一些它失败的领域，特别是因为实施的速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And once again, that process is sort of predicated on the assumption that implementation is expensive and that you can really only afford to build once.
zh: 再一次，这个过程是以实施很昂贵为前提的假设，你真的只能负担构建一次。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you need to fully, exhaustively go through the problem space and the solution space before implementing.
zh: 所以你需要彻底地、详尽地过一遍问题空间和解决方案空间，然后再实施。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then we saw with Figma and Origami and all of these tools that you can fast-forward some of the insights by pulling interactive prototypes earlier into the process.
zh: 然后我们看到通过 Figma 和 Origami 和所有这些工具，你可以通过更早在过程中引入交互式原型来快进一些洞察。

speaker: Unknown
en: That you can simulate production.
zh: 你可以模拟生产。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there ended up being sort of a meme about executives just being like, "Well, can we just do a prototype and then expect it to work?"
zh: 最后出现了一个关于高管的梗，就像是，“好吧，我们能不能做一个原型然后指望它能用？”

speaker: Unknown
en: But this thing was real that this became part of the design process proper.
zh: 但这件事是真实的，这成为了正统设计过程的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: We pulled prototyping into that.
zh: 我们把原型制作拉进了那个过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: The problem now is that you can pull all of the implementation into that and there's a mismatch between, I think a lot of assumptions.
zh: 现在的问题是你可以把所有的实施都拉进那个过程，我认为很多假设之间存在错位。

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, you see this fully polished prototype that looks like it's ready to go out the door.
zh: 同样，你看到这个完全打磨过的原型，看起来像是准备好要出门了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And enough people at a company see that and they're like, "Can we release this now?"
zh: 公司里足够多的人看到了那个，他们就像是，“我们现在能发布这个吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: But the appropriate, we're actually in that early design process stage and nobody's just saying that.
zh: 但是适当的，我们实际上处于早期设计过程阶段，没有人只是这么说。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is where we are with a bunch of multiplayer exploration.
zh: 这就是我们目前所处的进行大量多人探索的阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: 90 people will have this idea.
zh: 90 个人会有这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It'll look really polished, but it's like, "No, this is actually..." That's the design process now.
zh: 它看起来非常精良，但这就像是，“不，这实际上是……”这就是现在的设计过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Tying the design process to mediums, media, that's the scary part.
zh: 将设计过程与媒介、媒体联系起来，这才是可怕的部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that designers have more tools now to do this process with.
zh: 也就是设计师现在有更多的工具来进行这个过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can put stuff into the current product and you can A/B test it or just use that as a prototype.
zh: 你可以把东西放进当前的产品中，你可以对它进行 A/B 测试，或者直接把它作为一个原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Many companies right now have this idea of a baby version of the product.
zh: 现在许多公司都有这种产品婴儿版的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like baby Cursor, you've seen this on Twitter.
zh: 就像是婴儿版 Cursor，你在 Twitter 上见过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have baby Codex, a dramatically simplified code base that approximates all of the interactions of the production app.
zh: 我们有婴儿版 Codex，这是一个大幅简化的代码库，它模拟了生产应用程序的所有交互。

speaker: Unknown
en: And therefore is a lot quicker to vibe code over.
zh: 因此用它进行 vibe coding 要快得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you can be like, "Well, what if the sidebar worked like this?
zh: 因为你可以像是，“好吧，如果侧边栏是这样运作的呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or what if a pane came in and had a group chat here?
zh: 或者如果一个面板进来并且在这里有一个群聊会怎样？

speaker: Unknown
en: What if X, Y, Z?"
zh: 如果 X、Y、Z 会怎样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's a huge tool that's part of the design process.
zh: 那是设计过程中的一个巨大工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to say that the design process is dead, I feel like it's both true and false.
zh: 所以要说设计过程已经死了，我觉得这既是对的也是错的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that if you are tied to the tools and the exact day-to-day specifics of the process, then yeah, it's dead.
zh: 也就是说，如果你被工具和过程的确切的日常细节所束缚，那么是的，它已经死了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not going to have a good time.
zh: 你不会过得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to throw the process out completely or throw the overlay of the process, the like, "Hey, we are at this point in the process."
zh: 但是要完全抛弃这个过程，或者抛弃过程的覆盖层，就像是，“嘿，我们在过程的这个点上。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That is still more important than ever.
zh: 这仍然比以往任何时候都重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's really interesting because you have a background in every function.
zh: 这真的很有趣，因为你有每个职能的背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: If people look at your LinkedIn, it's like engineer, designer, product manager, founder.
zh: 如果人们看你的 LinkedIn，就像是工程师、设计师、产品经理、创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you oversee the desktop app.
zh: 现在你负责桌面应用程序。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think design is not under your purview, is that right?
zh: 而且我认为设计不在你的职权范围内，对吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there a separate design team or are they under your?
zh: 是有一个独立的设计团队，还是他们在你的下面？

speaker: Unknown
en: Depends on the week.
zh: 取决于这一周。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: We worked very closely together.
zh: 我们非常紧密地一起工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: We believe in, while sitting together, being embedded.
zh: 我们相信坐在一起，嵌入其中。

speaker: Unknown
en: Reporting line, I don't know.
zh: 汇报线，我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: They shift weekly.
zh: 它们每周都在变动。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does the design process look like on the Codex app?
zh: Codex 应用程序上的设计过程是什么样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, there's been a lot written about role collapse, existential role collapse.
zh: 是的，关于角色崩溃、存在性角色崩溃已经有很多文章了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are no roles anymore.
zh: 不再有角色了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We haven't seen that.
zh: 我们还没看到那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We have seen more role collapse in the Codex org than I think other parts of the company and other parts of the economy.
zh: 我们在 Codex 组织中看到了比我认为的公司其他部分和经济其他部分更多的角色崩溃。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think part of this is that this was a technical product for engineers.
zh: 我认为部分原因是这是一个面向工程师的技术产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so our designers speak engineer, our product managers speak technical language and write code.
zh: 所以我们的设计师说工程师的语言，我们的产品经理说技术语言并且写代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: Alexander has a master's degree in computer science, which is, I do not have a master's degree in computer science.
zh: Alexander 有计算机科学硕士学位，这，我没有计算机科学硕士学位。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we've seen a lot of role collapse.
zh: 所以我们看到了很多角色崩溃。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that one of the ways that we describe how the groups work together is that there's significantly more overlap in the roles than there used to be.
zh: 我认为我们描述团队如何协同工作的方式之一是，角色之间的重叠比以前多得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everybody's sort of defined less by the fence and the boundaries of where design stops and engineering starts, but more the average of where they're working.
zh: 每个人的定义越来越少地取决于设计停止和工程开始的围栏和边界，而更多地取决于他们工作的平均值。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you average up all of the things that somebody on our design team does, there's plenty of code writing things.
zh: 所以如果你平均一下我们设计团队某个人做的所有事情，有很多写代码的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's plenty of things that are product work, but on average there are dots over here if you draw it out on a diagram.
zh: 有很多是产品工作的事情，但平均来说如果你在图表上画出来，点主要在这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this sort of speaks to the process too, especially because the entirety of the Codex app has been informed by the dog fooding loop.
zh: 这也说明了这个过程，特别是因为整个 Codex 应用程序都是由 dog fooding 循环所告知的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There is a desire among all of us to try to do as much as possible in the app even when it's not the best tool, so that it can become the best tool.
zh: 我们所有人都有一种渴望，即使当它不是最好的工具时，也要在应用程序中尝试做尽可能多的事情，这样它就能成为最好的工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so a lot of design we all work on by using the app and say, "Okay, what's broken about this?"
zh: 所以我们都在做的很多设计工作是通过使用应用程序并说，“好吧，这有什么问题？”

speaker: Unknown
en: This is a whole thing we do, which is that we often don't improve our process so that we can make the product better to do it, which is a deeply uncomfortable place to be in.
zh: 我们做的这完全是另一回事，就是我们经常不改进我们的过程，以便我们可以让产品更好地去做这件事，这是一个非常让人不舒服的境地。

speaker: Unknown
en: But week to week, it's changing.
zh: 但是每周都在变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love this point so much that what are you?
zh: 我非常喜欢这一点，你到底是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's your role is the average of what you spend your time on.
zh: 你的角色是你花时间做的事情的平均值。

speaker: Unknown
en: If most of your work is PM-y work, then okay, you're PM for now.
zh: 如果你大部分工作是 PM 类工作，那么好吧，你现在就是 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: If it's engineering, you're an engineer for now.
zh: 如果是工程，你现在就是工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like, was OpenAI the first company to call people member of technical staff?
zh: 我觉得，OpenAI 是第一家把人称为 technical staff 成员的公司吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: No, I believe that this might've started with Xerox.
zh: 不，我认为这可能始于 Xerox。

speaker: Unknown
en: First company I interned at, it was a company called Upthere, did the same thing.
zh: 我实习的第一家公司，是一家叫 Upthere 的公司，做了同样的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: This has been around.
zh: 这种情况已经存在一段时间了。

speaker: Unknown
en: But it's much more common now, but it is kind of a tradition in research focused companies, right?
zh: 但现在它更普遍了，但这在以研究为重点的公司中是一种传统，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay, got it.
zh: 好的，明白了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it emerged from research.
zh: 所以它源于研究。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I feel like it's such a, I don't know, sign of where things might be headed, this idea of we're just going to call everyone member of technical staff, your function isn't set.
zh: 但我觉得这就像一个，我不知道，事情可能发展方向的迹象，这个我们要把所有人都称为 technical staff 成员，你的职能没有设定的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're not in this bucket of the PM org or the eng org or the design org.
zh: 你不在 PM 组织或工程组织或设计组织的这个桶里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like that's where we all head long-term?
zh: 你觉得这就是我们长期发展的方向吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like functions will continue to exist?
zh: 你觉得职能会继续存在吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: There's still the PM skillset and the eng skillset and the design skillset.
zh: 仍然有 PM 技能集、工程技能集和设计技能集。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people are, "I'm a designer."
zh: 人们是，“我是一名设计师。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you think this is like, people call it builder.
zh: 还是你认为这就像，人们称之为 builder。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you feel like there's this collapse coming where everyone's everything and that's just the future?
zh: 你觉得会有这种崩塌即将到来，每个人都是做所有事情的，这就是未来吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or do you think we're going to continue to be mostly divided up in quadrants?
zh: 还是你认为我们会继续主要被分成四个象限？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are some things that I'm afraid of.
zh: 有些事情是我担心的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that some companies like to be very extreme about getting onto the bandwagon of whatever people say is going to happen.
zh: 我认为有些公司喜欢非常极端地追随人们说将要发生的任何事情的潮流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think part of the danger in eliminating the concept of roles is that it can dangerously eliminate the idea that things are specialties with knowable best practices.
zh: 我认为消除角色概念的危险在于，它可能危险地消除这样一种想法，即事物是具有已知最佳实践的专长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've heard a lot of companies be like, "We're getting rid of the product role," which I think is by the way, a terrible idea.
zh: 我听过很多公司说，“我们要废除产品角色，”顺便说一句，我认为这是一个糟糕的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everybody's just going to be a builder.
zh: 而每个人都将只是 builder。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what happens is this whole discipline of product that's been built up and has real best practices, real things that have been tried and failed, and real processes, that just gets abandoned.
zh: 然后发生的是，整个产品学科已经建立起来，拥有真正的最佳实践，真正尝试过并失败过的事情，以及真正的流程，就这样被抛弃了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because people are like, "Oh, I wrote some code."
zh: 因为人们就像，“哦，我写了一些代码。”

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not a great place to be in.
zh: 那不是一个很好的处境。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that the boundary of so and-so, this isn't your lane.
zh: 我认为某某的界限，这不是你的赛道。

speaker: Unknown
en: I welcome that part going away.
zh: 我欢迎那部分消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's a balance here where it's like not everyone can work on everything, for one, both in terms of breadth and depth rate.
zh: 但这里有一个平衡，就像不是每个人都可以工作在所有事情上，首先，无论是在广度和深度比率上。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is why managers are not going to go away.
zh: 这就是为什么经理们不会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: Not everybody can work on everything.
zh: 不是每个人都可以工作在所有事情上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And also every discipline has a skill component to it, which I think a lot of engineers are guilty of not recognizing that, "Well, engineering has a skill to it.
zh: 而且每个学科都有一个技能组成部分，我认为很多工程师有一种不承认这种倾向的罪过，“嗯，工程是有技能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's writing code and other roles are just people vibing."
zh: 它是写代码，而其他角色只是人们 vibe coding。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, that's not how it works."
zh: 就像，“不，不是那样运作的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes, you can use Excel, but you cannot work on the finance team, that kind of stuff, right?
zh: 是的，你可以使用 Excel，但你不能在财务团队工作，那种事情，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's also just like, "Do you want to be doing this work?"
zh: 我认为还有就像，“你想做这项工作吗？”

speaker: Andrew Ambrosino
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do I want to be?
zh: 我想做吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think more of it's that actually now, is it's easier to switch roles.
zh: 我认为现在更多的是，实际上，现在更容易切换角色。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easier to learn the best practices.
zh: 更容易学习最佳实践。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's easier to not tie your effectiveness in a role with the ability to use the exact tool.
zh: 更容易不把你在一个角色中的有效性与使用确切工具的能力联系起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's more of like, "Can you get yourself into this mindset, learn which things work and which don't, and then focus on it?"
zh: 更像是，“你能让自己进入这种心态，学习哪些事情有效哪些无效，然后专注于它吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I spent so long feeling like I should not be a software engineer because I didn't care about assembly language or memorizing type scripts and texts.
zh: 我花了很长时间觉得我不应该是一名软件工程师，因为我不关心汇编语言或背诵 type scripts 和文本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like there have always been parts to these roles that are that sort of gatekeeping.
zh: 就像这些角色一直有那种守门的部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: That are like, "Well, no, being good at this role is being good at this tool."
zh: 就像，“嗯，不，擅长这个角色就是擅长这个工具。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's what's kind of starting to erode.
zh: 我认为这就是开始受到侵蚀的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think people take this, they hyperbolize all of this.
zh: 我认为人们接受了这个，他们夸大了这一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does your team look like on the Codex team?
zh: Codex 团队的团队构成是怎样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: How many engineers, designers, PMs?
zh: 有多少工程师、设计师、产品经理（PM）？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's kind of the makeup of the team right now?
zh: 目前团队的构成大致是怎样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Every time people ask me how many people are on the Codex team, do you remember my answer to this?
zh: 每次人们问我 Codex 团队有多少人，你记得我的回答吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "It's somewhere between 10 and a few thousand."
zh: 我会说，“大概在 10 到几千人之间吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's like a fake answer, but it's real in that we do see this as the culmination of what everybody works on here.
zh: 我是说，这听起来像个玩笑答案，但它真实之处在于，我们确实将其视为这里每个人工作的结晶。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything that goes into model research, everything that goes into how models are good at CUA and browser use.
zh: 投入到模型研究中的一切，投入到模型如何擅长 CUA 和浏览器使用中的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything about how model personality.
zh: 关于模型个性的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of the product work around front end infrastructure, all of the user, all of it is this product.
zh: 围绕前端基础设施的所有产品工作，所有的用户，所有这一切构成了这个产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the same time, we are not accepting PRs daily from thousands and thousands of people on whatever they want.
zh: 同时，我们并没有每天接受成千上万的人针对他们想做的任何东西提交的 PR。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we got a team, double digits of engineers, probably half that on the design side.
zh: 所以我们有一个团队，工程师数量是两位数，设计方面大概是工程师的一半。

speaker: Unknown
en: A few product people, although product here is kind of more of a zone defense play.
zh: 有一些做产品的人，尽管这里的产品工作更像是区域防守打法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think one thing that is very common among everybody on the Codex side or on the desktop side is agency and taste, right?
zh: 我认为在 Codex 方面或桌面方面，每个人共有的一个非常普遍的特点就是主观能动性和品味，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of former founders or people who were at larger companies doing founder shaped things.
zh: 很多前创始人，或者是在大公司里做创始人型事情的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of people with immense taste.
zh: 很多极具品味的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: At OpenAI, we let teams get very large.
zh: 在 OpenAI，我们允许团队变得非常大。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we haven't said, "Hey, there's no management," but the teams are quite large.
zh: 所以我们并没有说，“嘿，这里没有管理”，但团队确实相当大。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's mostly ICs and I think that's good.
zh: 大部分是独立贡献者（ICs），我认为这很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: You use this term zone defense for product work and that's really interesting.
zh: 你用区域防守这个词来形容产品工作，这真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It kind of maps to the design kind of shift also, just like you're there to manage and coordinate.
zh: 它某种程度上也对应了设计方面的转变，就像你在那里是为了管理和协调。

speaker: Unknown
en: Talk a little bit more about what that looks like.
zh: 多谈谈那是什么样子的。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does zone defense look like for a product person?
zh: 对于一个做产品的人来说，区域防守是什么样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I have had a lot of conversations with Alexander about this analogy, which is that if two product people are working too closely, that's often not a good signal.
zh: 我和 Alexander 就这个比喻进行过很多次讨论，那就是如果两个产品负责人合作得太紧密，这通常不是一个好信号。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that you kind of want, as a product org, you sort of want to do this force directed activity.
zh: 作为产品组织，你有点想要做这种力导向的活动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where you're like, "Where are the gaps?"
zh: 就像你在问，“缺口在哪里？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Especially in this new world where curation and steering and alignment is a lot of things.
zh: 特别是在这个新世界里，策展、引导和Alignment是很多东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where you're like, "There's a ton of chaos happening on people throwing ideas all over the place."
zh: 就像人们在到处扔想法，发生了大量的混乱。

speaker: Unknown
en: The whole top down, year long planning thing, not going to work.
zh: 那种自上而下、为期一年的规划方式是行不通的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so now it's like we need the taste makers to guide things from inception to what the product should be.
zh: 所以现在我们需要有品味的人来引导事物从萌芽到产品应有的样子。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that means you basically want company coverage.
zh: 这意味着你基本上想要覆盖全公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so you spread out and you say, "All right, who's best at what?
zh: 所以你分散开来说，“好吧，谁最擅长什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's create some space between us so that we got full coverage."
zh: 让我们在彼此之间创造一些空间，这样我们就有了完整的覆盖。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's kind of how it goes.
zh: 大致就是这样运作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you fill in the gaps and you're like, "Look, we want to hire engineers who are product-minded."
zh: 然后你填补空白，你会说，“看，我们想雇那些有产品思维的工程师。”

speaker: Unknown
en: We don't want it to be that we've got a bunch of people writing a bunch of code that needs full team reviewing it for product coherence.
zh: 我们不想要一大堆人写一堆代码，然后需要整个团队来审查它以确保产品的一致性。

speaker: Unknown
en: We want everyone to have these skills, but I think what people go deep on has to change.
zh: 我们希望每个人都拥有这些技能，但我认为人们深入钻研的方向必须改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is definitely a thread I've been noticing over and over with talking to folks like you.
zh: 这确实是我通过和你这样的人交谈一遍又一遍注意到的一个趋势。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is the most valuable person right now, one of the most valuable, is someone that could take an idea from idea to done with the taste to know this is great.
zh: 现在最有价值的人之一，是那种能够凭借“知道这很棒”的品味，把一个想法从想法变成成品的人。

speaker: Andrew Ambrosino
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just shepherding throughout, this obsession with making it awesome.
zh: 就像全程的引导，这种对让它变得很棒的痴迷。

speaker: Unknown
en: This kind of high agency, high taste person exactly as you described.
zh: 这种高主观能动性、高品味的人，完全就像你描述的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that kind of the way you think about here's who we're hiring, here's who's going to do really well in this new world?
zh: 这是否就是你思考“我们要雇佣谁”、“谁在这个新世界里会表现出色”的方式？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think that's the core piece right now.
zh: 是的，我认为这是现在的核心部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it also speaks to how I sort of see IC versus management, which is that it's not that management is going away.
zh: 这也说明了我是如何看待独立贡献者（IC）与管理的，那就是管理并没有消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not that everyone's an IC, but everyone's kind of both now.
zh: 并不是说每个人都是 IC，但现在每个人某种程度上都是两者兼修。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you are an IC, you're not typing code out character by character.
zh: 如果你是一个 IC，你不是在逐字逐字地敲代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are managing something.
zh: 你在管理某些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're managing agents.
zh: 你在管理智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: You are managing work that is happening that comes together to do a certain thing.
zh: 你在管理工作，这些工作汇聚在一起来完成某件特定的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you're a manager of teams, you're doing the same thing just at a different granularity.
zh: 如果你是团队的经理，你在做同样的事情，只是处于不同的粒度。

speaker: Unknown
en: I generally look for obviously command over the discipline, but then the taste to say, "Hey, you're going to have unlimited tokens and we can't just be doing slop."
zh: 我通常寻找明显对学科的掌控，但还要有品味去说，“嘿，你将拥有无限的 token，我们不能只做垃圾。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You need to be able to determine what signal, what's noise in a world of just infinite content.
zh: 你需要能够在一个只有无限内容的世界里确定什么是信号，什么是噪声。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mention planning.
zh: 提一下规划。

speaker: Unknown
en: At the pace things are moving, it's become very hard to plan roadmaps.
zh: 以事情发展的速度，制定路线图变得非常困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: I imagine, especially in your world.
zh: 我想象，特别是在你的世界里。

speaker: Unknown
en: People are very frustrated with me all the time on this.
zh: 人们总是对此对我感到非常沮丧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yes.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because things are just constantly shifting, things are changing.
zh: 因为事物在不断转变，情况在变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you plan on your team?
zh: 你的团队如何做规划？

speaker: Unknown
en: How far ahead are you thinking and what does a plan look like?
zh: 你会提前思考多远，计划看起来是什么样的？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it a spreadsheet?
zh: 是一个电子表格吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it MD file?
zh: 是一个 MD 文件吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the output of a plan?
zh: 计划的产出是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I don't think we do anything revolutionary on that.
zh: 是的，我认为我们在那方面没做任何革命性的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not clever about planning.
zh: 我们不擅长规划。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the basic gist is, the shorter term something is, the more detail it needs.
zh: 我认为基本的要点是，事情越短期，它需要的细节就越多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it's not that we don't plan for nine months out.
zh: 然后并不是说我们不制定九个月后的计划。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's that that just has to stay very hazy.
zh: 而是那必须保持非常模糊。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because any amount of precision that you add to a nine-month plan right now is false precision and you're just going to waste time.
zh: 因为任何你现在添加到九个月计划中的精确度都是虚假的精确度，你只是在浪费时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can say stuff, but nothing that we planned.
zh: 你可以说一些东西，但没有什么是我们计划好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think research is different.
zh: 我认为研究是不同的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm not speaking for research here, but on the applied side when we do product, anything that you could have planned in November may have been true for December, but isn't what happened.
zh: 所以我在这里不代表研究，但在应用侧当我们做产品时，任何你在 11 月可能已经计划好的事情可能在 12 月是真实的，但并不是实际发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's hard.
zh: 所以这很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is really hard to do planning.
zh: 做规划真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: We generally need to know what do we think models are able to do on what timeline?
zh: 我们通常需要知道我们认为模型在什么时间线上能够做什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: And my last company, I kind of saw this shift where we were starting to use the models to drive features.
zh: 在我上一家公司，我有点看到了这种转变，我们开始使用模型来驱动功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the product process fell down.
zh: 产品流程崩溃了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It basically had to be like, "Let's list out all of the things that we think we are interested in doing for the next year or two."
zh: 基本上必须是这样，“让我们列出我们所有感兴趣的、在未来一两年内要做的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's prototype all of them, decide which things are ready now, and then just let the others sit and bake.
zh: 让我们把它们全部原型化，决定哪些东西现在已经准备好了，然后就让其他的放在那里酝酿。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then every time there's like a new leap in models, let's try that thing again with it swapped out.
zh: 然后每次模型有了新的飞跃，让我们把它换掉后再试一次那个东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the whole premise of whether features were good or not were based on whether they were smart enough, not the shape of them.
zh: 因为功能是否好的整个前提是基于它们是否足够聪明，而不是它们的形状。

speaker: Unknown
en: So this is a great story about the Codex app.
zh: 所以这是一个关于 Codex 应用程序的很棒的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am very confident that the Codex app that we released in February, if that had been ready in November, it would've absolutely failed in the market.
zh: 我非常有信心，我们在 2 月发布的 Codex 应用程序，如果它在 11 月准备好了，它肯定会在市场上失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the only difference was the models between November and February.
zh: 唯一的区别是 11 月到 2 月之间的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think there's a lot to that, that this product with the exact same shape, I think its outcomes were totally different depending on just a few months of timing.
zh: 我认为这很有道理，这个产品具有完全相同的形状，我认为它的结果完全不同，仅仅取决于几个月的时机。

speaker: Unknown
en: This episode is brought to you by Mercury, radically different banking loved by over 300,000 entrepreneurs and now with Command.
zh: 本节目由 Mercury 赞助，这是一种截然不同的银行业务，受到超过 30 万企业家的喜爱，现在推出了 Command。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been a customer of Mercury's for over six years.
zh: 我做 Mercury 的客户已经超过六年了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have never once thought about leaving.
zh: 我从没想过要离开。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is basically what happens when banking is built by product people, not by bankers.
zh: Mercury 基本上是当银行业务是由产品人员而不是银行家构建时会发生的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: They make it so easy, dare I say, fun to send invoices, move money around, set up virtual cards for folks on my team.
zh: 他们让它变得如此简单，我敢说，发送发票、转移资金、为我的团队成员设置虚拟卡很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: Does your bank have an API, a terminal native CLI, or an AI-ready MCP server?
zh: 你的银行有 API、终端原生 CLI 或 AI 就绪的 MCP 服务器吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think so.
zh: 我不这么认为。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just recently they launched Command, a conversational interface built directly into Mercury, which acts as your financial operator.
zh: 就在最近他们推出了 Command，这是一个直接构建到 Mercury 中的对话界面，充当你的财务运营者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been using Command to transfer money around, to figure out what categories I've been spending the most money in, analyze my cash flows.
zh: 我一直在使用 Command 来转移资金，弄清楚我在哪些类别上花的钱最多，分析我的现金流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just today I used it to find out how much I've made from a specific sponsor over the past year.
zh: 就在今天我用它来找出过去一年我从某个特定赞助商那里赚了多少钱。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just ask, "How much have I made from X over the past year?"
zh: 我只是问，“过去一年我从 X 那里赚了多少钱？”

speaker: Unknown
en: 10 seconds later, I have an answer.
zh: 10 秒钟后，我就有了答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so freaking cool.
zh: 这真是太酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Visit mercury.com to learn more and apply online in minutes.
zh: 访问 mercury.com 了解更多信息并在几分钟内在线申请。

speaker: Unknown
en: Mercury is a FinTech company, not an FDIC insured bank.
zh: Mercury 是一家金融科技公司，而非 FDIC 承保的银行。

speaker: Unknown
en: Banking services provided through Choice Financial Group and Column NA, members FDIC.
zh: 银行服务通过 Choice Financial Group 和 Column NA 提供，这两者是 FDIC 的成员。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is definitely a thread on this podcast, is build things that are not yet working then will work when the model gets better.
zh: 这绝对是这期播客的一个主题，就是构建那些尚未能运作的东西，然后等到模型变好时它们就能运作了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's this kind of other thread of ambition, be more ambitious with the things you take on.
zh: 还有另一种雄心的主线，就是对你承担的事情要更有雄心。

speaker: Unknown
en: So is this just the way you approach things?
zh: 所以这只是你处理事情的方式吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You're just like, "Let's just build a bunch of things that may not work yet.
zh: 你就像，"咱们就构建一堆可能还不能运作的东西吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll just have them around and wait for a model to catch up."
zh: 我们把它们放在那里，等着模型赶上来。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that kind of the approach?
zh: 这是那种方法吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I think we have a lot of that.
zh: 是的，我想我们有很多这样的情况。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think sometimes the challenge is you have to be very clear again about what stage of the design process that's in.
zh: 我想有时挑战在于你必须再次非常清楚这处于设计过程的哪个阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: People still have this muscle memory of like, "Oh, I wrote the code for this thing, therefore we should put it out there."
zh: 人们还是有那种肌肉记忆，就像，"噢，我为这东西写了代码，所以我们就该把它发布出去。"

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, no, no."
zh: 这就像，"不，不，不。"

speaker: Unknown
en: That means you have an artifact now that we can test against for into future models.
zh: 那意味着你现在有了一个我们可以针对未来模型进行测试的工件。

speaker: Unknown
en: This happens with the in-app browser in the app that we have.
zh: 这发生在我们应用内的应用内浏览器上。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had a kind of a working version.
zh: 我们有一个可用的版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, go back to Atlas, we had agent working inside of Atlas and that was pretty cool.
zh: 我的意思是，回到 Atlas，我们在 Atlas 里有能工作的智能体，那很酷。

speaker: Unknown
en: We had Operator before that in ChatGPT.
zh: 在那之前我们在 ChatGPT 里有 Operator。

speaker: Unknown
en: That didn't work out.
zh: 那行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: Very cool idea.
zh: 很酷的主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's some thread that you can draw between Operator, Atlas, Codex, ChatGPT that's fundamentally the same feature.
zh: 在 Operator、Atlas、Codex、ChatGPT 之间有一条你可以画的线，那基本上是同一个功能。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the re-releasing of it with different intelligence totally changes the outcome here.
zh: 但是用不同的智能重新发布它，完全改变了这里的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I push people not to be stubborn about like, "No, this isn't working so it's a bad feature."
zh: 所以我推动人们不要固执己见，就像，"不，这不行，所以这是个坏功能。"

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, "No, it might not be ready yet."
zh: 这就像，"不，它可能还没准备好。"

speaker: Unknown
en: There's also this aspect of, especially in research, there's always a desire to be the most ambitious.
zh: 还有另一方面，特别是在研究中，总有一种想要最雄心勃勃的愿望。

speaker: Unknown
en: And to say, "Okay, but at the limit the model can just do this."
zh: 并且会说，"好的，但在极限情况下模型就是能做这个。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And that just doesn't work on the product side.
zh: 而这在产品方面行不通。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you go back to the original Codex release, basically what it was is it said, it was Codex web and it wasn't good for interacting with.
zh: 如果你回到最初的 Codex 发布，基本上它就是，它是 Codex web，它不适合用来交互。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was like, you give the model a task and it's going to go off, do the task, come back to you with it finished.
zh: 它就像，你给模型一个任务，它就会去执行任务，然后带着完成的任务回到你这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't sound that radical.
zh: 这听起来没那么激进。

speaker: Unknown
en: The problem is it didn't do the task that well.
zh: 问题是它没把任务做得那么好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It wrote code, it was good, but it was like that form factor was too early.
zh: 它写代码，很好，但就像那个外形因子太早了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the Claude Code comes out.
zh: 然后 Claude Code 出来了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Totally local, not hooked up to the cloud, doesn't pretend to be as...
zh: 完全本地，不连到云端，不假装那么...

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not as AGI-pilled.
zh: 它没那么 AGI-pilled。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to ask you questions, it's going to sit there.
zh: 它会问你问题，它会待在那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't just delegate your life to it.
zh: 你不能就把你的生活委托给它。

speaker: Unknown
en: That worked way better because that's the point that the models were out there.
zh: 那好使得多，因为那就是模型当时所处的阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we were too AGI-pilled for the moment.
zh: 所以我们要在那一刻太 AGI-pilled 了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think about that lesson a lot on this stuff.
zh: 我在这些东西上经常思考那个教训。

speaker: Unknown
en: It used to be that failing in market told you all these things about the shape of the product, about the communication of the product.
zh: 过去在市场上失败会告诉你关于产品形状、关于产品沟通的所有这些事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now it's like, "No, you might need to release this thing six different times before it works."
zh: 现在就像，"不，你可能需要在不同时间发布这个东西六次，它才行得通。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And the shape might not change at all.
zh: 而且形状可能根本不会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's so interesting to hear about all the variables you have to think about building product now.
zh: 听到你们现在构建产品必须考虑的所有变量真是太有意思了。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's the timeline for the models and the research and how smart it gets.
zh: 有模型和研究的时间线，以及它变得有多智能。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's people's ability to even understand this is how you could build software in the cloud and this is the future.
zh: 有人们理解这其实是你可以在云端构建软件的方式，而且这是未来的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: Get people prepared for this new feature and then just what you can build as a team.
zh: 让人们为这个新功能做好准备，然后就是你们作为一个团队能构建什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I love that Codex example because it comes back to this idea of ambition.
zh: 我喜欢那个 Codex 的例子，因为它回到了雄心这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I want to hear if there's anything there for you, of just this thread of just be more ambitious because these models can do so much more than you even imagine.
zh: 我想听听那里对你来说有没有什么，就是这种要更有雄心的主线，因为这些模型能做的比你能想象的还要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes it's too ambitious for the market and they're not ready for it.
zh: 而有时对市场来说太雄心了，他们还没准备好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But you think about that at all, just pushing your team to be more ambitious because it's so much easier to just do things that maybe felt crazy hard in the past.
zh: 但你考虑那个吗，就是推动你的团队更有雄心，因为做那些过去可能感觉难如登天的事情现在容易得多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, this is a core challenge.
zh: 是的，这是一个核心挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once there's a product that exists or a feature that exists, it's really easy for people to find paper cuts and-
zh: 一旦一个产品存在或一个功能存在，人们就很容易发现微小的问题和

speaker: Unknown
en: Micro-optimize.
zh: 微优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And they should.
zh: 他们应该这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And people on Twitter like to remind us of this and I thank them for that.
zh: Twitter 上的人们喜欢提醒我们这一点，我对此表示感谢。

speaker: Unknown
en: People should be focused on the features that exist and make them more reliable and better.
zh: 人们应该专注于现有的功能，并使它们更可靠、更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: But this is why we also have a culture of bottoms up exploration here.
zh: 但这就是为什么我们这里也有一种自下而上的探索文化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because sometimes in the same way that Codex app came and disrupted ChatGPT in some way, this thing will get disrupted by a future effort.
zh: 因为有时就像 Codex 应用出现并以某种方式颠覆了 ChatGPT 一样，这个东西也会被未来的工作所颠覆。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's part of the design is that.
zh: 这也是设计的一部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't always as one team be good at both the disruptive piece instead of maintaining a product and its quality piece.
zh: 作为一个团队，你不可能总是既擅长颠覆性的部分，又擅长维护产品及其质量。

speaker: Unknown
en: At some point, you have to design a process that allows for both.
zh: 在某个时候，你必须设计一个允许两者并存的过程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Kind of zooming out a little bit.
zh: 稍微拉远一点视角。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about the progression that we've been on of AI impacting how we build product, it's insane how far we've come from.
zh: 如果你考虑我们在 AI 影响我们构建产品方面的进展，我们要走的距离简直是疯狂的。

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, we used to write all our code by hand, artisanally created human code.
zh: 正如你所说，我们以前曾经手工编写所有代码，人工精心制作代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: To AI writing 100% of our code, to you actually put it this way that now coding is steering the AI.
zh: 到 AI 编写我们 100% 的代码，到你实际上用这种方式来表达，现在编码是在引导 AI。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you think about what percentage of my code is written by AI, it's almost like how many times did I have to steer it in the right direction as the AU version of coding?
zh: 当你考虑我的代码有多少百分比是由 AI 编写的时，这几乎就像作为编码的 AU 版本，我不得不在多少次将它引导到正确的方向？

speaker: Unknown
en: And now that there's agents and loops and all these things, what's kind of the latest frontier from what you've seen of how people are building?
zh: 现在有了智能体和循环以及所有这些东西，根据你所见，人们构建方式的最新前沿是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is it loops?
zh: 是循环吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is there something else of just the most AI-pilled, AI forward teams, here's how they operate now that people may not be aware of.
zh: 还有其他什么吗，对于那些最 AI 化、最 AI 优先的团队，这就是他们现在的运作方式，而人们可能没有意识到。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, loops are so last week, man.
zh: 我的意思是，循环已经是上周的事了，伙计。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, we talked about this.
zh: 我的意思是，我们谈论过这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: One of the big questions is always, "Well, how much of the product is AI written?"
zh: 最大的问题之一总是，“嗯，产品有多少是 AI 编写的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's always hard to answer that question because if you're using the goalposts from last year, it's like, "Well, 100% of our product right now is AI written code."
zh: 这个问题总是很难回答，因为如果你使用去年的标准，这就像，“嗯，我们现在 100% 的产品都是 AI 编写的代码。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So the question is more like, "Well, okay, fine.
zh: 所以问题更像是，“嗯，好吧，好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is the code written supervised versus unsupervised?"
zh: 代码是在监督下编写的还是在无监督下编写的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's a totally different thing.
zh: 这完全是两码事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I welcome the moving of goalposts because that means we're making product progress.
zh: 我欢迎这种标准的变化，因为这意味着我们在产品上取得了进步。

speaker: Unknown
en: There have been a lot of explorations here around autonomously developed software.
zh: 这里有很多关于自主开发的软件的探索。

speaker: Unknown
en: A lot of harness engineering stuff, a lot of different explorations on like, "Okay, well, what if you came in overnight and did garbage collection of the code base to clean it up?"
zh: 很多 harness 工程类的东西，很多不同的探索，比如，“好吧，如果你在晚上进来对代码库进行垃圾回收清理它会怎样？”

speaker: Unknown
en: One thing that I think all models suffer with right now is just they usually increase complexity.
zh: 我认为所有模型目前都面临的一个问题是，它们通常会增加复杂性。

speaker: Unknown
en: If research is listening at any company, please make the models better at deleting code.
zh: 如果任何公司的研究人员正在听，请让模型更擅长删除代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: But that becomes a problem right now when you try to put development completely on autopilot and it's both on the human side and the code base side.
zh: 但当你试图将开发完全置于自动驾驶模式时，这就会成为一个问题，这既涉及人员方面也涉及代码库方面。

speaker: Unknown
en: So feature requests.
zh: 所以功能请求。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you teach a model which features to build, which ones to ignore, which ones to group together and reframe a little bit?
zh: 你如何教一个模型要构建哪些功能，忽略哪些功能，将哪些功能组合在一起并稍微重构一下？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you teach to model how to build the right abstractions?
zh: 你如何教模型如何构建正确的抽象？

speaker: Unknown
en: All of this is getting better.
zh: 所有这一切都在变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think we're at place yet where we're like, "We're just going to set up a loop that's like improve the app."
zh: 我认为我们还没有到一个地方，我们会说，“我们要建立一个像改进应用这样的循环。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And listens to Twitter and listens to Slack and listens to email.
zh: 并且监听 Twitter、监听 Slack、监听邮件。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're not there yet, but we're trying to make it happen.
zh: 我们还没到那一步，但我们正在努力实现它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we'll get there?
zh: 你认为我们会到那里吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you think we'll get to a place where it's just like grow, win?
zh: 你认为我们会到一个只需增长、获胜的地方吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Slash goal, make money, make me a billion dollars.

zh: 这些都有很多权衡。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not super well-traveled path.
zh: 这不是一条走得很多很顺的路。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most browsers are browsers at the top level.
zh: 大多数浏览器在顶层就是浏览器。

speaker: Unknown
en: They've got browser tabs.
zh: 它们有浏览器标签页。

speaker: Unknown
en: This creates a lot of really boring but tedious problems like keyboard shortcuts, right?
zh: 这产生了很多真正无聊但琐碎的问题，比如键盘快捷键，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are we trying to do key mapping to VS code or to Chrome or to our own thing?
zh: 我们试图做按键映射到 VS code 还是到 Chrome 还是我们自己的东西？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or to Linear?
zh: 还是到 Linear？

speaker: Unknown
en: We want to have some sort of muscle memory that carries over, but got all these things that have sub-shapes of different products out on the market.
zh: 我们想要某种能转移过来的肌肉记忆，但是所有这些东西在市场上都有不同产品的子形态。

speaker: Unknown
en: What do we do?
zh: 我们该怎么办？

speaker: Unknown
en: And this just highlights how extra challenging this app is where you have to allow it to work.
zh: 这只是突显了这个应用有多么额外的挑战性，你必须允许它工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: For somebody that's never built anything from the more basic user, to power like Peter, OpenClaw trying to code with it.
zh: 对于那些从未构建过任何东西的更基本的用户，到像 Peter 这样的强力用户，OpenClaw 试图用它来编码。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm not convinced I'm going to get Peter to use the app.
zh: 我不相信我会让 Peter 使用这个应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think he might be the last-
zh: 我想他可能是最后一个——

speaker: Unknown
en: He's a terminal hold out.
zh: 他是一个终端的坚守者。

speaker: Andrew Ambrosino
en: ... terminal hold out.
zh: ... 终端的坚守者。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm going to keep trying.
zh: 但我会继续尝试。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let me zoom out for a moment and talk about the big picture of where you're taking all this.
zh: 让我稍微拉远一点视角，谈谈你们所有这些工作的宏观方向。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the vision for Codex?
zh: Codex 的愿景是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Where does this go?
zh: 它要走向何方？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's it going to look like, I don't know, a year or two, 10 years?
zh: 我不知道一年、两年还是十年后，它会是什么样子？

speaker: Unknown
en: We had Codex as a CLI and then we decided to build this app.
zh: 我们先有 Codex 这个 CLI，然后我们决定构建这个应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we were a little uncertain about the app but had a lot of conviction in what it could be as to start a developer tool.
zh: 我们对这个应用有点不确定，但对于它作为一个开发者工具的起点有着很强的信念。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it wasn't going to be an ITE, it was going to be this right sized surface.
zh: 它不会是一个 ITE，它会是一个尺寸合适的界面。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where it was sort of a chatbot but it was more than that and you could see the code but we weren't going to let you edit the code.
zh: 它有点像聊天机器人，但比那更多，你可以看到代码，但我们不会让你编辑代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a really interesting thing that happened at OpenAI in January and February.
zh: 1 月和 2 月在 OpenAI 发生了一件非常有趣的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it was before we actually released the Codex app, which is that we'd started to dog food the Codex app.
zh: 那是在我们真正发布 Codex 应用之前，也就是我们已经开始在内部试吃 Codex 应用的时候。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what we were finding, we were converging on some pretty clear internal PMF on engineering and research workflows.
zh: 我们的发现是，我们在工程和研究工作流上正在达成一些非常清晰的内部 PMF。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were thrilled.
zh: 他们非常激动。

speaker: Unknown
en: They were loving it.
zh: 他们非常喜欢它。

speaker: Unknown
en: We were like, "All right, we just got to get the quality bar up before we release it to the world.
zh: 我们心想，“好吧，在向世界发布之前，我们只需要把质量标准提上去。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're convinced that this will be a thing."
zh: 我们确信这会成事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But then at the company, we spun up a few other workflows to say, "Hey, this Codex effort is onto something with these coding agents."
zh: 但随后在公司里，我们启动了几个其他工作流，说，“嘿，这个 Codex 项目在这些 coding agents 上是有点东西的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And we have people from marketing, from comms, from finance, from legal, from basically every discipline, who are using this Codex app, even though it is actively hostile to these people.
zh: 我们有来自市场、传播、财务、法律，基本上每个学科的人，都在使用这个 Codex 应用，即使它对这些人来说是积极敌对的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is trying to show them code.
zh: 它试图向他们展示代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's trying to ask for approval to run RG on the...
zh: 它试图请求批准去在……上运行 RG

speaker: Unknown
en: It's doing all of these things that are actively not the right product surface for them.
zh: 它做的所有这些事情，对他们来说都是完全错误的产品界面。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why don't we take our other surfaces and add Codex to them?
zh: 那我们为什么不把我们的其他界面拿来，并给它们加上 Codex？

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's add it to the ChatGPT desktop app.
zh: 让我们把它加到 ChatGPT 桌面应用上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Let's add it to the Atlas browser.
zh: 让我们把它加到 Atlas 浏览器上。

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's essentially take the lessons of Codex and make it more general for a general knowledge work tool.
zh: 让我们基本上吸取 Codex 的教训，并让它对一个通用知识工作工具更通用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those efforts went for a little bit.
zh: 这些工作进行了一段时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the most annoying problem happened, which is nobody would leave the Codex app for the apps that were allegedly for these other personas.
zh: 然后最恼人的问题发生了，就是没有人愿意为了那些据称是给这些其他角色用的应用而离开 Codex 应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the lesson in all of this was just that the whole developer tool versus general knowledge work tool, there's a lot of nuance here that isn't just one or the other.
zh: 我认为这一切的教训只是，整个开发者工具与通用知识工作工具之间，有很多细微之处，不仅仅是非此即彼。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think we believe really strongly in this.
zh: 我认为我们要坚定地相信这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that there are certainly, in the same way that we talk about the average of your role as what your role is now, this is true on the product side too.
zh: 而且肯定存在，就像我们谈论你角色的平均值就是你现在的角色一样，这在产品方面也是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: People who are doing Excel work don't want to see Git repository information.
zh: 做 Excel 工作的人不想看到 Git 仓库信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: We know that, but we also know that we can tell a lot from what they're doing about what kind of work they do.
zh: 我们知道这一点，但我们也知道我们可以从他们正在做的事情中看出他们是做哪类工作的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we can start simple, grow the product to complex as we feel is needed.
zh: 我们可以从简单开始，根据需要让产品变得复杂。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't mean we don't have modes.
zh: 这并不意味着我们没有模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: You might want some modes for organizing your stuff and to sort of be legible about the ways that you enter the experience.
zh: 你可能需要一些模式来整理你的东西，并让进入体验的方式更加清晰。

speaker: Unknown
en: But we really believe strongly that what we've built here is the right shape to take on really deep, vertically focused things.
zh: 但我们非常坚信，我们在这里构建的是正确的形状，可以承担真正深度的、垂直专注的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: We work deeply with our finance team, with our team working on science, team working on legal.
zh: 我们与我们的财务团队、我们的科学团队、我们的法律团队深入合作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we say, "If we can build the right extensibility primitives in the right general model, then you can do anything with this."
zh: 我们说，“如果我们能在正确的通用模型中构建正确的可扩展性原语，那么你可以用这个做任何事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then our challenge is, well, how do you generalize it?
zh: 然后我们的挑战是，好吧，你如何泛化它？

speaker: Unknown
en: But this is kind of going back to the best desktop app that we can build.
zh: 但这有点回到构建我们能构建的最好的桌面应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: What does that look like?
zh: 那会是什么样子？

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it was Codex the developer tool, ChatGPT, where's this going?
zh: 所以 Codex 这个开发者工具，ChatGPT，这是要往哪里去？

speaker: Unknown
en: This is how we think about it.
zh: 这就是我们的思考方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so interesting, the point you made that the Codex app was so, you did such a good job getting people to be aware it existed.
zh: 你刚才提到的这一点很有意思，Codex 应用是如此，你做得很好，让人们知道它的存在。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so good to use and fun to use that everyone's starting to use that versus the ChatGPT app.
zh: 而且非常好用、很有趣，以至于大家都开始使用它而不是 ChatGPT 应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So clearly the direction is combining them so that you're not creating this confusion, which I know is things people have been talking about, this idea of come bringing them together.
zh: 显然方向是将它们结合起来，这样你就不会制造这种混乱，我知道人们一直在谈论这一点，这个把它们带到一起的想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Somebody called it a super app and wish they hadn't said that because now I have to hear about the super app all day, every day.
zh: 有人把它称为超级应用，并希望自己没说过，因为现在我不得不每天、每时每刻都听到关于超级应用的事。

speaker: Unknown
en: We'll get past it.
zh: 我们会跨过去的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great.
zh: 很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: But is that kind of the... Let's not call it a super app, but the idea is one place people go to do all the things.
zh: 但这有点像……咱们别叫它超级应用，但想法是人们去一个地方做所有的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that the general idea?
zh: 这是大致的想法吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Or TBD?
zh: 还是待定？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think what we see here is that it's a great home base.
zh: 我认为我们在这里看到的是它是一个很好的大本营。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a great place to keep track of all of the things that you have to do across different surfaces.
zh: 这是一个很好的地方来跟踪你必须在不同的界面上做的所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: And some of those things, you do all of it in the app.
zh: 其中有些事情，你完全在应用里做。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some of those things the app opens other apps to do.
zh: 其中有些事情，应用打开其他应用来做。

speaker: Unknown
en: The app can connect to Excel so that yes, it has a spreadsheet editor inside the app.
zh: 应用可以连接到 Excel，所以是的，它在应用内部有一个电子表格编辑器。

speaker: Unknown
en: Is that good enough for people doing financial modeling at OpenAI for raising billions of dollars?
zh: 这对于那些在 OpenAI 做财务模型工作、筹集数十亿美元的人来说够好吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Probably not.
zh: 可能不够。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the app talks directly to the add-in Microsoft Excel on your desktop.
zh: 所以应用直接和你桌面上的 Excel 加载项对话。

speaker: Unknown
en: When it's done, you can close Excel.
zh: 做完后，你可以关闭 Excel。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so it's not just about, "Hey, we're drawing a rectangle on the screen and everything needs to happen in that rectangle."
zh: 所以这不仅仅是关于，“嘿，我们在屏幕上画一个矩形，一切都得在那个矩形里发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's, "This thing should be a home for you where you start work, you end work, you automate work, and it uses whatever you need to do."
zh: 而是，“这东西应该是一个你的家，你开始工作，结束工作，自动化工作，它使用你需要做的任何事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a great story about, we had some videos that we shot in this room for the original launch of the Codex app.
zh: 有一个很棒的故事，我们在这个房间里拍摄了一些视频，用于 Codex 应用的最初发布。

speaker: Unknown
en: And our in-house DX videographer, Brent, then was tasked with editing all these videos.
zh: 然后我们的内部 DX 摄像师 Brent 接到了编辑所有这些视频的任务。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he edited all the videos with Codex, which was one of the early like, "Whoa, what are people doing with this thing?"
zh: 他用 Codex 编辑了所有视频，这是早期的那些“哇，人们正在用这东西做什么？”的时刻之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the process for why he decided to start using Codex was really interesting.
zh: 关于他决定开始使用 Codex 的过程真的很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: He started just because he was curious if Codex could edit videos.
zh: 他开始只是因为好奇 Codex 是否能编辑视频。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so Codex is not a video editor per se.
zh: 所以 Codex 本身并不是一个视频编辑器。

speaker: Unknown
en: It doesn't have any of that UI in it, but it was able to understand that he used Premiere Pro.
zh: 它里面没有任何那个 UI，但它能够理解他使用了 Premiere Pro。

speaker: Unknown
en: It could do some edits by editing the files that were backing what was on-screen in Premiere Pro, but it couldn't do everything.
zh: 它可以通过编辑支持 Premiere Pro 屏幕内容的文件来做一些编辑，但它不能做所有事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So naturally what Codex then did was built itself an extension that could be installed into Premiere Pro.
zh: 所以自然而然 Codex 接下来做的是为自己构建了一个可以安装到 Premiere Pro 中的扩展。

speaker: Unknown
en: That it could then talk to and say, "Hey, Premiere Pro extension, can you please change this marker inside of the Premier Pro app?"
zh: 然后它可以与之对话并说，“嘿，Premiere Pro 扩展，能不能请你修改 Premiere Pro 应用里的这个标记？”

speaker: Unknown
en: That was pretty nuts when we saw that happening.
zh: 当我们看到这种情况发生时，真是太疯狂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a great model.
zh: 这是一个很棒的模型。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are these specialty tools that specialize in things.
zh: 有这些专门处理某事的专用工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we're trying to do two things at once with Codex and with now with ChatGPT.
zh: 所以我们试图同时用 Codex，现在也用 ChatGPT 做两件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is how can we seamlessly interact with these tools that you're already using?
zh: 一是我们如何与你已经在使用的这些工具无缝交互？

speaker: Unknown
en: And say, "We don't need to build a better video editor for you."
zh: 并说，“我们不需要为你构建一个更好的视频编辑器。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But Codex and ChatGPT can use that video editor.
zh: 但 Codex 和 ChatGPT 可以使用那个视频编辑器。

speaker: Unknown
en: It can interact again, can hand stuff off to it.
zh: 它可以再次交互，可以把东西交给它。

speaker: Unknown
en: So how can we do that?
zh: 那么我们如何做到这一点呢？

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's often through connectors or computer use or even extensions in this case.
zh: 这通常是通过连接器或计算机使用，或者在这个案例中甚至是扩展来实现的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there is Dan Shipper's thing, which is, "Hey, I have these web apps that you can click around and use, but I want to be able to open these in Codex and have Codex do extra stuff with it."
zh: 然后还有 Dan Shipper 的东西，就是，“嘿，我有这些你可以点击和使用的 Web 应用，但我希望能够打开它们并在 Codex 里让 Codex 用它们做额外的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so those are kind of like two models that are almost inverse of each other that we're doing a lot with both at the same time.
zh: 所以这些是两种几乎互逆的模型，我们同时在两者上做了很多工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: This Premiere story is interesting to me because it's another example of just be more ambitious with these AI jobs.
zh: 这个 Premiere 的故事对我来说很有趣，因为它是另一个对这些 AI 工作更有雄心的例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: You may not know, maybe they could do this thing.
zh: 你可能不知道，也许它们能做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's almost just like, "Go try, go try out, see if it figures it out."
zh: 几乎就像是，“去试试，去试试，看它能不能弄明白。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm going to take us to a recurring corner on the podcast that I call Fail Corner.
zh: 我要带我们去播客的一个常设板块，我称之为失败角。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the question for you is people see people like you just killing it, just growing.
zh: 所以给你的问题是，人们看到像你这样的人简直是大杀四方，一直在成长。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everything's winning.
zh: 一切都在赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: Codex is doing so great, this crazy career, everything's up and to the right.
zh: Codex 做得太好了，这疯狂的职业生涯，一切都蒸蒸日上。

speaker: Unknown
en: People may not see the times that things didn't work out and things that you launched that were failures.
zh: 人们可能看不到那些事情不成功的时候，以及你推出的那些失败的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so these stories are really important for people to hear, that it's not all just win all the time.
zh: 所以这些故事对人们听到真的很重要，这并不一直全是赢。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a story of a time you failed in your career that taught you something really important?
zh: 有没有一次你职业生涯中失败并教会你一些重要事情的故事？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's funny to hear that description played back at me.
zh: 听到那个描述回放给我听很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: And this is perhaps the first time I've not felt like I was failing.
zh: 这也许是我第一次不觉得我在失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I was a startup founder for a long time.
zh: 我的意思是，我做了很久的初创公司创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I ended up selling the company for parts essentially.
zh: 我最终基本上把公司拆散卖了。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was years, it was a slog, it was heavily regulated spaces.
zh: 那是好几年，那是苦差事，那是受到严格监管的领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: The whole thing felt like a constant failure.
zh: 整件事感觉就像不断的失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: I went to this other startup and we were trying to do some AI tools in this also pretty locked down, regulated industry.
zh: 我去了另一家初创公司，我们试图在这个同样相当封闭、受监管的行业做一些 AI 工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that felt like just time after time of trying things and it not working.
zh: 那感觉就像是一次又一次地尝试东西却不起作用。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me it's been like, "Oh, I've failed actually quite a lot."
zh: 所以对我来说，就像是，“哦，我实际上失败了很多次。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And sometimes it's just a point in time where things line up like skillset, passion, point in the market, line up.
zh: 有时这只是事情在某个时间点对齐了，比如技能组合、激情、市场时机，都对齐了。

speaker: Unknown
en: With this project to bring what we've learned with the Codex app and marry it with ChatGPT, there have been, I don't know how many micro failures on this.
zh: 随着这个项目，把我们在 Codex 应用中学到的东西与 ChatGPT 结合起来，已经发生了，我不知道有多少次微小的失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: Where we're like, "This is the shape it should look like."
zh: 我们觉得，“这应该是它该有的样子。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then throw that in Slack and there's like a 2,000 message thread about how stupid we are.
zh: 然后把它扔进 Slack，那里有一个大约 2000 条消息的线程，说我们要有多蠢。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the thing I love about OpenAI, is people will just tell us that.
zh: 这就是我喜欢 OpenAI 的地方，人们会直接告诉我们那些。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's no holding back on when we fail with product things internally.
zh: 当我们在内部产品上失败时，没有任何隐瞒。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's why the external product has been pretty great is because it goes through these cycles of-
zh: 这就是为什么外部产品一直相当不错，因为它经历了这些周期——

speaker: Unknown
en: 2,000, this sucks.
zh: 2000 条，这太烂了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I failed for, I don't know, somewhere between 10 and 15 years before getting to this point.
zh: 我失败了，我不知道，大概 10 到 15 年才到达这个阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm still surprised every day that things are going well.
zh: 所以我仍然每天都很惊讶事情进展顺利。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know it.
zh: 我知道这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think this is really important for people to hear.
zh: 我认为这对人们来说真的很重要。
